rationale.R1.2: The artifact makes non-trivial correctness claims: linearizability under crash failures, leader-lease-bounded in-flight operations, and a sequence-number-based leader election. 'Check this' on a protocol description centrally implicates consistency verification — e.g., whether the lease mechanism is actually sufficient to avoid stale reads, and whether the election rule is safe under concurrent failures.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete quantitative claims appear (87k vs 62k vs 58k ops/sec framed as a '40% improvement'; median latency 3.1 ms; 30-day deployment at 80k ops/sec). Checking these for internal arithmetic consistency and plausibility against the described experimental setup is a natural, central responsibility given the artifact.
